title: Adobe Patches Actively Exploited Zero-Day That Lingered for Months
summary: An attacker has been using maliciously crafted PDF files to exploit a zero-day in Adobe Acrobat and Reader for at least four months.

sections.overview: An attacker has been using maliciously crafted PDF files to exploit a zero-day in Adobe Acrobat and Reader for at least four months.
sections.technical: An attacker has been using maliciously crafted PDF files to exploit a zero-day in Adobe Acrobat and Reader for at least four months.
sections.mitigation: Apply vendor-supplied patches as soon as possible. If patching is not immediately feasible, implement recommended workarounds and compensating controls.
sections.context:
Zero-day vulnerabilities represent the highest-priority threats as they are exploited before vendors can issue patches. Rapid detection and compensating controls are essential.

Timely vulnerability management and patch prioritization remain critical components of any defensive security program.

email_summary: An attacker has been using maliciously crafted PDF files to exploit a zero-day in Adobe Acrobat and Reader for at least four months. Severity is rated critical.

title: Your MTTD Looks Great. Your Post-Alert Gap Doesn't
summary: Anthropic restricted its Mythos Preview model last week after it autonomously found and exploited zero-day vulnerabilities in every major operating system and browser. Palo Alto Networks' Wendi Whitmore warned that similar capabilities are weeks or months from proliferation. CrowdStrike's 2026 Global Threat Report puts average eCrime breakout time at 29 minutes. Mandiant's M-Trends

sections.overview: Anthropic restricted its Mythos Preview model last week after it autonomously found and exploited zero-day vulnerabilities in every major operating system and browser. Palo Alto Networks' Wendi Whitmore warned that similar capabilities are weeks or months from proliferation. CrowdStrike's 2026 Global Threat Report puts average eCrime breakout time at 29 minutes.
sections.technical: Anthropic restricted its Mythos Preview model last week after it autonomously found and exploited zero-day vulnerabilities in every major operating system and browser.
sections.impact:
Assessed severity: CRITICAL.

Systems running affected software versions are at risk until patches or mitigations are applied.
sections.mitigation: Apply vendor-supplied patches as soon as possible. If patching is not immediately feasible, implement recommended workarounds and compensating controls. Review endpoint detection rules and update threat intelligence feeds. Scan for known indicators of compromise.
sections.context:
Zero-day vulnerabilities represent the highest-priority threats as they are exploited before vendors can issue patches. Rapid detection and compensating controls are essential.

Timely vulnerability management and patch prioritization remain critical components of any defensive security program.

Modern malware campaigns frequently use multi-stage delivery chains and evasion techniques. Defense-in-depth strategies including EDR, network monitoring, and application allowlisting help reduce exposure.

title: Why Orgs Need to Test Networks to Withstand DDoS Attacks During Peak Loads
summary: Security teams can't test distributed denial-of-service defenses in a vacuum. They need to test during periods of high demand, such as tax filing deadlines.

title: CISA Adds Seven Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
summary: CISA has added seven new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation.

sections.overview:
CISA has added seven new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2012-1854 Microsoft Visual Basic for Applications Insecure Library Loading Vulnerability

 CVE-2020-9715 Adobe Acrobat Use-After-Free Vulnerability

 CVE-2023-21529 Microsoft Exchange Server Deserialization of Untrusted Data Vulnerability

 CVE-2023-36424 Microsoft Windows Out-of-Bounds Read Vulnerability

 CVE-2025-60710 Microsoft Windows Link Following Vulnerability

 CVE-2026-21643 Fortinet SQL Injection Vulnerability

 CVE-2026-34621 Adobe Acrobat and Reader Prototype Pollution Vulnerability

 
 These types of vulnerabilities are frequent attack vectors for malicious cyber actors and pose significant risks to the federal enterprise. Binding Operational Directive (BOD) 22-01: Reducing the Significant Risk of Known Exploited Vulnerabilities established the KEV Catalog as a living list of known Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) that carry significant risk to the federal enterprise.
sections.technical:
CVE-2012-1854 Microsoft Visual Basic for Applications Insecure Library Loading Vulnerability

 CVE-2020-9715 Adobe Acrobat Use-After-Free Vulnerability

 CVE-2023-21529 Microsoft Exchange Server Deserialization of Untrusted Data Vulnerability

 CVE-2023-36424 Microsoft Windows Out-of-Bounds Read Vulnerability

 CVE-2025-60710 Microsoft Windows Link Following Vulnerability

 CVE-2026-21643 Fortinet SQL Injection Vulnerability

 CVE-2026-34621 Adobe Acrobat and Reader Prototype Pollution Vulnerability

 
 These types of vulnerabilities are frequent attack vectors for malicious cyber actors and pose significant risks to the federal enterprise. CISA has added seven new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. Binding Operational Directive (BOD) 22-01: Reducing the Significant Risk of Known Exploited Vulnerabilities established the KEV Catalog as a living list of known Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) that carry significant risk to the federal enterprise. Although BOD 22-01 only applies to FCEB agencies, CISA strongly urges all organizations to reduce their exposure to cyberattacks by prioritizing timely remediation of KEV Catalog vulnerabilities as part of their vulnerability management practice.

Referenced vulnerabilities: CVE-2012-1854, CVE-2020-9715, CVE-2023-21529, CVE-2023-36424, CVE-2025-60710.
sections.impact:
CVE-2012-1854 Microsoft Visual Basic for Applications Insecure Library Loading Vulnerability

 CVE-2020-9715 Adobe Acrobat Use-After-Free Vulnerability

 CVE-2023-21529 Microsoft Exchange Server Deserialization of Untrusted Data Vulnerability

 CVE-2023-36424 Microsoft Windows Out-of-Bounds Read Vulnerability

 CVE-2025-60710 Microsoft Windows Link Following Vulnerability

 CVE-2026-21643 Fortinet SQL Injection Vulnerability

 CVE-2026-34621 Adobe Acrobat and Reader Prototype Pollution Vulnerability

 
 These types of vulnerabilities are frequent attack vectors for malicious cyber actors and pose significant risks to the federal enterprise. Binding Operational Directive (BOD) 22-01: Reducing the Significant Risk of Known Exploited Vulnerabilities established the KEV Catalog as a living list of known Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) that carry significant risk to the federal enterprise. Although BOD 22-01 only applies to FCEB agencies, CISA strongly urges all organizations to reduce their exposure to cyberattacks by prioritizing timely remediation of KEV Catalog vulnerabilities as part of their vulnerability management practice.
sections.mitigation:
BOD 22-01 requires Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies to remediate identified vulnerabilities by the due date to protect FCEB networks against active threats.

Apply vendor-supplied patches as soon as possible. If patching is not immediately feasible, implement recommended workarounds and compensating controls. Review endpoint detection rules and update threat intelligence feeds. Scan for known indicators of compromise.
sections.context:
Timely vulnerability management and patch prioritization remain critical components of any defensive security program.

Modern malware campaigns frequently use multi-stage delivery chains and evasion techniques. Defense-in-depth strategies including EDR, network monitoring, and application allowlisting help reduce exposure.

email_summary: CISA has added seven new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. Tracked as CVE-2012-1854, CVE-2020-9715, CVE-2023-21529.

title: OpenAI Revokes macOS App Certificate After Malicious Axios Supply Chain Incident
summary: OpenAI revealed a GitHub Actions workflow used to sign its macOS apps led to the download of the malicious Axios library on March 31, but noted that no user data or internal system was compromised.

sections.overview: OpenAI revealed a GitHub Actions workflow used to sign its macOS apps led to the download of the malicious Axios library on March 31, but noted that no user data or internal system was compromised.

title: North Korea's APT37 Uses Facebook Social Engineering to Deliver RokRAT Malware
summary: The North Korean hacking group tracked as APT37 (aka ScarCruft) has been attributed to a fresh multi-stage, social engineering campaign in which threat actors approached targets on Facebook and added them as friends on the social media platform, turning the trust-building exercise into a delivery channel for a remote access trojan called RokRAT.

sections.mitigation: Reinforce security awareness training. Ensure email filtering, link scanning, and multi-factor authentication are enabled. Review endpoint detection rules and update threat intelligence feeds. Scan for known indicators of compromise.

Phishing remains the most common initial access vector. Security awareness training combined with technical controls like email filtering and MFA are key defenses.

Modern malware campaigns frequently use multi-stage delivery chains and evasion techniques. Defense-in-depth strategies including EDR, network monitoring, and application allowlisting help reduce exposure.

title: APT41 Delivers 'Zero-Detection' Backdoor to Harvest Cloud Credentials
summary: The prolific China-backed threat group is targeting AWS, Google, Azure, and Alibaba cloud environments and using typosquatting to obscure C2 communication.

title: Empty Attestations: OT Lacks the Tools for Cryptographic Readiness
summary: OT asset owners are being asked by regulators to attest to their post-quantum cryptographic readiness without the appropriate tooling, resulting in paperwork dressed up to look like genuine security.

title: FBI and Indonesian Police Dismantle W3LL Phishing Network Behind $20M Fraud Attempts
summary: The U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), in partnership with the Indonesian National Police, has dismantled the infrastructure associated with a global phishing operation that leveraged an off-the-shelf toolkit called W3LL to steal thousands of victims' account credentials and attempt more than $20 million in fraud.

title: JanelaRAT Malware Targets Latin American Banks with 14,739 Attacks in Brazil in 2025
summary: Banks and financial institutions in Latin American countries like Brazil and Mexico have continued to be the target of a malware family called JanelaRAT.

sections.overview:
Banks and financial institutions in Latin American countries like Brazil and Mexico have continued to be the target of a malware family called JanelaRAT. A modified version of BX RAT, JanelaRAT is known to steal financial and cryptocurrency data associated with specific financial entities, as well as track mouse inputs, log keystrokes, take screenshots, and collect system metadata.
